extra.message: SQL injection vulnerability: User input from params['id'] is concatenated directly into SQL query without parameterization. Attacker can inject arbitrary SQL.
SQL injection vulnerability: User input from params['comment'] is concatenated directly into INSERT statement without parameterization. Attacker can inject SQL.

SQL injection vulnerability: User input from params.get('password') is concatenated directly into SQL WHERE clause without parameterization. Although username is filtered with regex, password is not sanitized. Attacker can inject SQL via password parameter.
Reflected XSS vulnerability: User input from params['v'] is substituted directly into HTML response via regex substitution without HTML encoding. Attacker can inject JavaScript.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: Reflected XSS via JSONP callback: User input from params['callback'] is directly concatenated into JSON response as function name without validation. Attacker can inject arbitrary code.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: Insecure deserialization: User input from params['object'] is directly deserialized using pickle.loads() without validation. This allows arbitrary code execution (RCE).

Path traversal / SSRF vulnerability: User input from params['path'] is used to open files or fetch URLs without proper validation. Attacker can read arbitrary files using path traversal (../) or fetch internal services via SSRF.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: Command injection vulnerability: User input from params['domain'] is concatenated into shell command passed to subprocess.run() with shell=True. Attacker can execute arbitrary OS commands.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command

extra.message: XPath injection vulnerability: User input from params['name'] is concatenated directly into XPath expression without escaping. Attacker can inject arbitrary XPath to access unauthorized data.

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data into an XPath Expression

extra.message: XML External Entity (XXE) injection: User input from params['xml'] is parsed with lxml parser configured with load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, and no_network=False. Attacker can exploit XXE for file disclosure or DoS.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: Denial of Service via resource exhaustion: User input from params['size'] is used to create strings with large repetitions without size validation. Attacker can cause memory exhaustion and crash the application.
Remote code injection / RFI vulnerability: User input from params['include'] is used to load and execute Python code via exec(). Attacker can execute arbitrary Python code or fetch remote files.

cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code

extra.message: Open redirect vulnerability: User input from params['redir'] is used in HTTP refresh meta tag without validation. Attacker can redirect users to malicious websites.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site

extra.message: HTTP header injection: User input from params.get('charset') is directly used in Content-Type header without validation. Attacker can inject HTTP headers.

cwe: CWE-113: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers
